generation.image_prompt: Mystical 1900s lithography tarot card illustration. Clean bold black ink outlines. NO BORDERS. FULL BLEED IMAGE. WIDE FIELD OF VIEW. ANTHROPOMORPHIC: A Taiwan leopard cat standing upright on two legs, head bandaged, holding one wooden wand firmly. Behind him, EXACTLY EIGHT more wooden wands are arranged in a narrow, dense semi-circle (Total 9 wands). All wands must be visible and close to the cat to avoid being cut off in vertical crop. Slender build, sharp facial structure, white ear spots, forehead stripes.
generation.narrative: An old leopard cat survivor, scarred by traps and roads, leans on its staff as a final wall of eight wands protects its core territory. It is the face of species resilience.

meanings.upright: Resilience, stamina, persistence, being nearing the end of a battle but still standing, and defensive strength.
meanings.reversed: Exhaustion, giving up, being too tired to defend any longer, or a system that has finally collapsed from fatigue.

ecology.risk_theme: the price of survival in human landscape
ecology.mapping_note: Nine of Wands is the 'Old General'—the individuals that manage to reach old age despite incredible habitat pressure, embodying the survival spirit.

website.excerpt: Scarred but unbowed, the old survivor stands his ground, leaning on his staff of experience.
website.seo_description: Nine of Wands reimagined as leopard cat resilience and the wisdom of the scarred survivor.

meaning.zh: Resilience, stamina, persistence, being nearing the end of a battle but still standing, and defensive strength.
meaning.en: Resilience, stamina, persistence, being nearing the end of a battle but still standing, and defensive strength.
